basic_details.job_title: DRDO DRDE Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Walkin
basic_details.organization_name: Defence Research and Development Establishment (DRDO DRDE)
basic_details.short_information: The Defence Research and Development Establishment (DRDO DRDE) has published the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Notification 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill unlimited Junior Research Fellow (JRF) positions. Candidates within the age range of up to 28 years (relaxable for SC/ST by 5 years and OBC by 3 years) and holding a first-class M.Sc. in Chemistry or Life Sciences/Zoology/Biotechnology/Molecular Biology/Microbiology/Pharmacology/Toxicology/Immunology with a valid CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR JRF Fellowship are eligible to attend the walk-in interview. The interviews will be conducted on 17 and

important_dates.reporting time: 09:30 hrs on the date of Interview

application_fee.details: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification.

age_limit.age_details: Max 28 years, relaxable for SC/ST (5 years) and OBC (3 years).
age_limit.details: Maximum age limit is 28 years. Age relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST candidates and 3 years for OBC candidates. Other categories will receive relaxation as per CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR Policy.

qualification.education_qualification: M.Sc. in Chemistry or Life Sciences/Zoology/Biotechnology/Molecular Biology/Microbiology/Pharmacology/Toxicology/Immunology with CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR JRF Fellowship.
qualification.details: Junior Research Fellow (JRF) with CSIR/UGC Fellowship: First class M.Sc. in Chemistry (Physical/Analytical/Organic/Inorganic) or related fields. Qualified & Valid Fellowship from CSIR/UGC (Qualified Joint CSIR-UGC-NET JRF Exam). Junior Research Fellow (JRF) with CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR Fellowship: First Class M.Sc. in Life Sciences/Zoology/ Biotechnology/ Molecular Biology/ Microbiology/ Pharmacology/ Toxicology/ Immunology or related fields. Qualified for JRF & presently held Valid Fellowship from CSIR/UGC/ICMR/DBT.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Junior Research Fellow (JRF) with CSIR/UGC Fellowship
Junior Research Fellow (JRF) with CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR Fellowship

category_wise_vacancy.Post Name: Junior Research Fellow (JRF) with CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR Fellowship

salary_details.details: Junior Research Fellow (JRF): Monthly Stipend and other allowances as per CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR Guidelines.

faq.question: Q1. What is the post name for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The post name for this recruitment is Junior Research Fellow (JRF).

faq.question: Q2. What is the mode of application for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The mode of application is Walk-in Interview.

faq.question: Q3. What is the maximum age limit for the JRF post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the JRF post is 28 years, with relaxations applicable for SC/ST and OBC categories.

faq.question: Q4. What are the educational qualifications required for the JRF post?
faq.answer: Candidates need an M.Sc. in Chemistry or Life Sciences/Zoology/Biotechnology/Molecular Biology/Microbiology/Pharmacology/Toxicology/Immunology with a valid CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR JRF Fellowship.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the JRF position?
faq.answer: The salary will be as per the guidelines of CSIR/UGC/DBT/ICMR.

faq.question: Q6. Where is the Defence Research and Development Establishment located?
faq.answer: Defence Research and Development Establishment is located in Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh.

faq.question: Q7. Are there any application fees for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q8. What documents should I carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: You should carry all original certificates, mark sheets, experience certificates (if any), caste certificate, and a duly filled application form.

faq.question: Q9. When is the walk-in interview scheduled?
faq.answer: The walk-in interviews are scheduled for 17 June 2026 (Chemistry JRF) and 18 June 2026 (Life Sciences JRF).

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the application form?
faq.answer: The application form (Annexure 'B') can be downloaded from the DRDO website.
